div.p: William by the grace of God king of Scots to the bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of all his land clerk and lay greeting. Let present and future know me to have granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks in that place serving God the donation which Robert of Berkeley and his spouse Cecilia the daughter of Liulf made to the same monks of the ploughgate of land in the territory of Maxton which the aforesaid Robert perambulated for the aforementioned monks and by the same bounds which are contained in the charter of the aforenamed Robert and his spouse Cecilia, and with all easements of the same toun as well in pastures as in easements of firewood, and stone of the quarry of Alwerdene sufficient for the building of the edifice of the monastery of Melrose, just as it is contained in the charter of the aforesaid. Wherefore I will and firmly enjoin that the aforesaid monks have and possess the aforementioned land with all the above-written easements in free and pure and everlasting alms as freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as any other alms in all my kingdom is as freely and quitely, fully and honourably held and possessed, and just as the charter of the aforesaid Robert and his spouse Cecilia bears witness and affirms; saving my service, and saving the teinds of the church of Maxton. As witnesses: Hugh my chancellor; Robert and Ralph my chaplains; Richard de Prebenda my clerk; Robert of London; William of Lindsay; Malcolm son of Earl Duncan; William Comyn; William de la Haye; William Giffard; Walter of Berkeley, chamberlain; Herbert Chalmers; AT FORFAR.
